findings.issue: MVP with 4 skills + 5 shared references + like-figma migration + do-task integration is still large for a single iteration
findings.why_matters: Even though size L is declared, the MVP alone contains: 4 SKILL.md files with procedural phases, 5 shared reference documents (design-tokens.md, component-patterns.md, generation-guide.md, design-principles.md, library-catalog.md), migration of content from like-figma, and a modification to do-task SKILL.md. This is a lot of markdown engineering in one pass. Risk of context exhaustion during implementation sessions.
findings.fix: At tech-spec stage, consider splitting MVP into two increments: (1) design-system-init + design-generate + shared references + like-figma migration, (2) design-review + design-retrospective + do-task integration. Each is independently testable. This is a tech-spec decomposition concern, not a user-spec blocker.

findings.issue: No specification for design-system evolution after initial creation
findings.why_matters: Scenario A covers creating a design system, but what if user wants to add a color, change a font, or add components later? like-figma had an 'Evolve' mode. Without this, users must re-run init (losing customizations) or manually edit tokens.json. This will come up quickly after first use.
findings.fix: Add a note in constraints or roadmap: 'MVP design-system-init supports re-run with update/overwrite choice (already covered in edge case: .design-system/ already exists). Granular evolution (add single token) — post-MVP.' This clarifies the interim workaround.

findings.issue: design-system-init interview mechanics are not specified
findings.why_matters: The methodology has a well-defined interview pattern (3 cycles, scoring, completeness checker, interview-templates). The spec says 'conducts interview: proposes specific values, user confirms/corrects' but doesn't say whether to reuse the existing interview infrastructure or use a simpler approach. This leaves a gap for the tech-spec author.
findings.fix: Add one sentence to Scenario A or Technical Decisions: 'design-system-init uses a simplified propose-and-confirm interview (not the full 3-cycle scoring pattern from user-spec-planning). Details in tech-spec.'

findings.issue: Library catalog (library-catalog.md) could benefit other skills beyond design-pipeline
findings.why_matters: A catalog of free icon/element libraries is useful for code-writing when building UI, not only for design-generate. Making it design-pipeline-specific limits reuse.
findings.fix: Consider placing library-catalog.md in a shared location. This is a tech-spec organization concern — note it for tech-spec author.

findings.issue: Contrast ratio check (AC #6: >= 4.5:1) requires color computation that may be complex for a markdown skill to instruct
findings.why_matters: Verifying WCAG AA contrast ratio requires parsing colors and computing relative luminance. The AC states it as a hard requirement but the skill is markdown instructions. The agent can approximate but precise computation needs a script.
findings.fix: Clarify in tech-spec whether this is a generation constraint (agent picks compliant colors) or a verification step (script in scripts/ directory). Not a user-spec blocker — the requirement itself is correct.

summary: All 5 critical/major issues from round 1 have been addressed: MVP now includes 4 skills with retrospective, edge cases are per-scenario, do-task integration mechanism is specified, SVG justification is provided, and design-retrospective is differentiated from /retrospective. Remaining findings are minor and appropriate for tech-spec to resolve. Spec is approved for tech-spec phase.
